products.name: Epiq Discover™
lab_concepts: Early Case Assessment with Epiq Assist™
recommendation: Skillable Simulations is the correct and only viable delivery path for Epiq Discover™ because the platform is a managed SaaS service with no self-hosted deployment option, no per-learner sandbox API, and no Azure/AWS Marketplace listing. Simulation via AI Vision captures the UI-driven workflows — ECA, document review, production configuration — that define the learner experience. The existing certification exam's MCQ format (65 questions, 85% pass threshold) can be embedded directly alongside simulation modules to create a blended assessment experience without requiring a real environment.

AI Vision screen-state validation is the primary scoring mechanism for simulation labs — confirming correct navigation, tagging decisions, and workflow completion steps. MCQ scoring is a confirmed and accepted method given the existing certification exam format. Performance-based testing (PBT) is not viable without a real environment API, but the combination of AI Vision + MCQ provides meaningful outcome validation for the certification use case.

The strongest entry point is enhancing the existing Epiq Discover End User Certification with simulation-based practice labs — because the certification already exists with a formal exam structure, adding hands-on practice labs as a prerequisite directly addresses the gap between passive video training and the 85% pass threshold. This is a low-friction conversation: Epiq already believes in certification; Skillable adds the hands-on practice layer that makes certification outcomes more reliable and defensible.

The key question that unblocks this engagement is whether Epiq has a dedicated training environment or tenant that could be used for simulation content authoring — and whether the team that owns the certification program has budget authority to add a lab platform. The team most likely to own this is whoever manages the Epiq Discover Certification program (likely within Product or Customer Success). A verbatim question your champion can send: 'Do we have a dedicated training or sandbox environment for Epiq Discover that our content team uses to build certification prep materials? And who owns the decision to add hands-on lab components to the certification program?'

The saas_only architecture is a persistent constraint — simulation is the correct path today, but it limits scoring depth and learner immersion compared to real environment labs. If Epiq ever exposes a per-learner sandbox API or a self-hosted deployment option, the Product Labability score would increase significantly and a real environment lab path would become viable. Monitor for API openness signals in future product releases, particularly as Epiq AI Labs (led by VP Bishan Yang) develops more platform capabilities.

dimensions.name: Provisioning
dimensions.summary: Epiq Discover™ is a managed SaaS platform with no self-hosted deployment option, no Azure/AWS Marketplace listing, and no evidence of a per-learner sandbox API. The only viable lab path is Skillable Simulations, which scores at the simulation tier (+12 base). The saas_only ceiling cap (18) applies to the overall Product Labability pillar.

evidence.claim: **Deployment Model | Context:** Epiq Discover™ is a cloud-hosted managed SaaS platform operated entirely by Epiq — no self-hosted, on-premises, or customer-deployed option is documented, making Skillable Simulations the only viable lab delivery path. — Epiq product page (epiqglobal.com). Simulation is the correct orchestration method when real provisioning is blocked by SaaS architecture.

**No Sandbox API | Risk:** No public REST API, developer tier, or per-learner sandbox provisioning mechanism is documented for Epiq Discover™ — the platform is provisioned by Epiq as a managed service for enterprise clients, with no self-service isolation path. — Epiq technology page (epiqglobal.com). Without a per-learner API, real environment provisioning is not feasible at lab scale.

**No Marketplace Listing | Risk:** No Azure Marketplace or AWS Marketplace listing for Epiq Discover™ was found, and no IaC templates (Terraform, Bicep, ARM, CloudFormation) are documented — ruling out Cloud Slice as a provisioning path. — Research findings (inferred from absence of marketplace evidence). Cloud Slice paths require either a marketplace listing or customer-deployable infrastructure templates.

dimensions.name: Lab Access
dimensions.summary: No credential pool, NFR license tier, or trial account confirmed. Access to Epiq Discover is provisioned by Epiq as a managed service for enterprise clients — no self-service or per-learner isolation mechanism exists. Simulation path bypasses credential management entirely but provides no real environment access.

evidence.claim: **No Trial Tier | Risk:** No free trial, developer account, or NFR license tier is documented for Epiq Discover™ — access is provisioned by Epiq directly for enterprise clients under contract, with no self-service onboarding path. — Epiq product page (epiqglobal.com). Absence of a trial tier means lab authoring requires direct vendor engagement to obtain a training environment.

**Enterprise Pricing Model | Risk:** Epiq Discover™ is priced as an enterprise managed service — no per-seat or consumption-based pricing is published, and per-learner provisioning cost at lab scale is unknown but likely prohibitive without a dedicated training environment agreement. — Epiq product page (epiqglobal.com). Enterprise managed service pricing structures typically require negotiated training environment access.

dimensions.name: Scoring
dimensions.summary: Simulation scoring via AI Vision is the primary method — screen state validation for UI-driven workflows. No public REST API for programmatic outcome validation confirmed. MCQ fallback is viable given the existing certification exam format (65-question multiple choice/true-false).

evidence.claim: **AI Vision Scoring | Context:** In simulation format, learner interactions with the Epiq Discover™ UI can be evaluated via AI Vision screen-state validation — confirming correct navigation, tagging decisions, and workflow completion steps. — Skillable platform capability (inferred from simulation scoring model). AI Vision is the primary scoring mechanism when no programmatic API surface exists.

**Certification Exam Format | Strength:** The existing Epiq Discover End User Certification uses 65 multiple-choice and true/false questions with an 85% passing threshold — confirming that MCQ-based knowledge validation is already an established and accepted scoring method for this product. — Epiq Discover Certification page (epiqglobal.com). MCQ scoring can be directly integrated into simulation labs as a validated fallback.
evidence.source_title: Epiq Discover™ End User Certification Page

dimensions.name: Teardown
dimensions.summary: Simulation environments have no persistent state — teardown is automatic and instantaneous. No orphan risk, no cleanup required between learner sessions.

evidence.claim: **Simulation Teardown | Strength:** Simulation environments maintain no persistent state — each session is stateless and teardown is automatic with zero cleanup overhead between learners. — Skillable platform architecture (inferred from simulation model). No orphan risk, no manual cleanup, no teardown API required.

dimensions.name: Product Complexity
dimensions.summary: Epiq Discover™ spans a multi-phase eDiscovery lifecycle: data collection and processing, early case assessment, AI-assisted document review with Continuous Active Learning, custodian management, tagging/redaction, and production. Epiq Assist™ adds an AI natural language querying layer requiring iterative practice. Multiple distinct role types (admin, reviewer, attorney, compliance officer) each interact with the platform differently. The workflow is genuinely complex — not learnable by watching.

evidence.claim: **End-to-End eDiscovery Pipeline | Strength:** Epiq Discover™ spans a complete multi-phase legal workflow — data collection and processing, early case assessment, AI-assisted document review with Continuous Active Learning, custodian management, privilege review, redaction, and production — each phase requiring distinct skills and configuration decisions. — Epiq Discover™ product page (epiqglobal.com). Multi-phase workflows justify a curriculum of labs, not just a single introductory module.

**Epiq Assist™ AI Features | Strength:** Epiq Assist™ enables natural language querying across entire datasets and powers Continuous Active Learning (CAL) that adapts to reviewer decisions over time — both features require iterative hands-on practice to use effectively and cannot be learned through passive video training. — Epiq Discover™ product page (epiqglobal.com). AI-embedded features that adapt to user behavior are a strong signal for hands-on lab demand.

**Multiple User Roles | Context:** The certification overview identifies distinct skill domains — administrative tasks, collection and processing, review workflows, and production — suggesting at least three distinct role types (admin, reviewer, legal ops) each requiring separate training tracks. — Epiq Discover Certification page (epiqglobal.com). Role diversity supports a multi-track lab program rather than a single course.
evidence.source_title: Epiq Discover™ End User Certification Page

dimensions.name: Mastery Stakes
dimensions.summary: eDiscovery is one of the highest-stakes domains in enterprise software. Misconfiguration — missed custodians, improper privilege designations, incomplete productions — can result in court sanctions, adverse inference instructions, privilege waiver, or regulatory penalties. The learning curve from novice to competent practitioner is steep, and poor platform adoption drives continued over-reliance on expensive outside counsel.

evidence.claim: **Legal Defensibility Risk | Strength:** eDiscovery errors — missed custodians, improper privilege designations, incomplete productions, or inadequate redactions — can result in court sanctions, adverse inference jury instructions, privilege waiver, or regulatory penalties; the platform explicitly markets 'defensibility' as a core value proposition. — Epiq Discover™ product page (epiqglobal.com). High-stakes consequences make hands-on practice essential, not optional.

**Outside Counsel Dependency | Strength:** Epiq Discover™ explicitly markets 'reduced reliance on external resources' as a key outcome — indicating that poor platform adoption drives continued over-reliance on expensive outside counsel, a documented and costly failure mode for legal operations teams. — Epiq Discover™ product page (epiqglobal.com). Adoption risk is a direct business case for hands-on training investment.

**Certification Complexity Signal | Context:** The End User Certification requires a Pre-Certification Course, covers administrative tasks through production workflows, and sets an 85% passing threshold — signaling that the platform has sufficient complexity to warrant formal credentialing and structured preparation. — Epiq Discover Certification page (epiqglobal.com). A formal certification with a high pass threshold confirms the learning curve is real.
evidence.source_title: Epiq Discover™ End User Certification Page

dimensions.name: Lab Versatility
dimensions.summary: Two high-value lab types apply: Compliance Audit (validating review configurations against regulatory and court requirements) and Break/Fix (diagnosing matter setup errors, processing failures, or workflow misconfigurations). Both are viable in simulation format.

evidence.claim: **Regulatory Review Validation | Opportunity:** Epiq Discover™ supports customizable document review tagging and categorization for state and local regulatory compliance — a natural fit for Compliance Audit labs where learners validate that review configurations meet specific regulatory or court-ordered requirements. — Epiq Discover™ product page (epiqglobal.com). Compliance Audit labs are high-value for legal ops and government entity personas.

**Matter Setup Troubleshooting | Opportunity:** eDiscovery workflows have multiple failure points — processing errors, custodian gaps, privilege log inconsistencies, production format mismatches — making Break/Fix labs viable for training reviewers and admins to diagnose and resolve common matter setup problems. — Epiq Discover™ product page (epiqglobal.com, inferred from workflow complexity). Break/Fix scenarios are particularly effective for building the diagnostic skills that prevent costly legal errors.

dimensions.name: Market Demand
dimensions.summary: Legal Tech is a moderate-demand category (+4). Epiq Assist™ AI features add a learning AI-embedded features signal (+3). The Fortune 100 / AmLaw 100 client base confirms a large enterprise install base (+2). The eDiscovery market is growing as data volumes and regulatory complexity increase (+1). No active competitor labs or certification body partnerships confirmed.

evidence.claim: **Fortune 100 / AmLaw 100 Client Base | Strength:** Epiq serves Fortune 100 companies, AmLaw 100 law firms, and G7 government departments — confirming enterprise-scale adoption and a large, high-value training population with budget authority for professional development. — Epiq company homepage (epiqglobal.com). Enterprise validation confirms the training population is real and resourced.

**Epiq Assist™ AI Integration | Opportunity:** Epiq Assist™ provides natural language querying and AI-powered early case assessment across entire datasets — AI features embedded in a legal workflow product that require hands-on iterative practice to use effectively, driving training demand beyond basic platform orientation. — Epiq Discover™ product page (epiqglobal.com). AI-embedded features in enterprise software consistently increase training demand and lab program scope.

**End User Certification Active | Strength:** Epiq Discover™ has a published End User Certification with a formal exam structure (65 questions, 85% pass threshold, 70-minute time limit) and a recommended Pre-Certification Course — confirming an active credentialing ecosystem that creates structured training demand. — Epiq Discover Certification page (epiqglobal.com). An active certification program is a direct entry vector for Skillable lab integration.
evidence.source_title: Epiq Discover™ End User Certification Page

dimensions.name: Training Commitment
dimensions.summary: Epiq has a confirmed End User Certification for Epiq Discover™ with a formal exam (65 questions, 85% pass threshold, 70-minute time limit). A Pre-Certification Course, Video Training Center, Getting Started videos, and Help documentation are published. Training investment is real but appears lean — no dedicated training organization or CLO identified. Regulated industry context (legal/compliance) inherently drives training demand.

evidence.claim: **Active Certification with Formal Exam | Strength:** The Epiq Discover End User Certification is a published, active program with a 65-question exam, 85% passing threshold, 70-minute time limit, and a recommended Pre-Certification Course — demonstrating real investment in structured learner validation, not just informal training resources. — Epiq Discover Certification page (epiqglobal.com). An active certification with a high pass threshold is the strongest signal of training commitment.

evidence.claim: **Self-Service Training Resources | Context:** Epiq publishes a Video Training Center, Getting Started videos, and a Help Page for Epiq Discover™ — a functional but lean training catalog that relies on self-service resources rather than a structured instructor-led or lab-based curriculum. — Epiq Discover Certification page (epiqglobal.com). Lean catalog signals an opportunity to expand training depth, not a mature program to integrate with.

evidence.claim: **Legal and Compliance Context | Context:** Epiq's client base operates in heavily regulated environments — federal courts, regulatory agencies, Fortune 100 legal departments — where defensible process documentation and demonstrated competence are not optional, creating inherent demand for structured training and certification. — Epiq company homepage (epiqglobal.com). Regulated industry context sustains training demand independent of vendor investment level.

dimensions.name: Organizational Dna
dimensions.summary: Epiq is a 30-year-old legal services company that has evolved into a technology provider — a services-first DNA with growing software investment. No formal reseller or partner program for Epiq Discover confirmed. Integration maturity is unclear — no public API documentation found. Large, established organization that may be complex to engage for a platform partnership.

evidence.claim: **30-Year Established Services Company | Context:** Epiq has operated for over 30 years serving Fortune 100 and AmLaw 100 clients — a large, established organization with complex internal structures that may require navigating multiple stakeholders to reach a training platform decision. — Epiq company homepage (epiqglobal.com). Large, established services companies are workable but require identifying the right internal champion.

**No Public API Documentation Found | Risk:** No public REST API documentation, developer portal, or SDK was identified for Epiq Discover™ — suggesting a closed platform architecture that may limit integration options for lab provisioning or scoring automation. — Research findings (inferred from absence of API documentation). Closed platform architecture is a risk for any future move beyond simulation to real environment provisioning.

**Services-First Technology Company | Context:** Epiq's core business is legal services delivery — technology is a supporting capability, not the primary revenue driver — suggesting a mixed build/buy orientation where external platform partnerships for training delivery are plausible but not the default posture. — Epiq company homepage (epiqglobal.com). Services-first companies often buy training platforms rather than build them internally.

Delivery capacity is the weakest dimension — labs would need to be delivered through Epiq's own channels.

evidence.claim: **No Partner Training Network | Risk:** No authorized training partner (ATP) network, reseller training program, or channel enablement structure was identified for Epiq Discover™ — training appears to be delivered directly by Epiq through self-service resources, limiting scale and reach. — Research findings (inferred from absence of partner program documentation). Without an ATP network, lab consumption is limited to direct Epiq customer and employee channels.

**No Current Lab Platform | Context:** No existing hands-on lab platform (Skillable, Instruqt, CloudShare, or equivalent) was identified in Epiq's training infrastructure — the certification program relies on a knowledge-based exam rather than performance-based testing, representing a greenfield opportunity. — Epiq Discover Certification page (epiqglobal.com). Greenfield lab platform opportunity — no incumbent to displace.

key_technical_questions.heading: Key Technical Questions
bullets: Tenant provisioning for training — Find the **Principal Engineer or Platform Architect on the Epiq Discover product team**; they own the multi-tenancy model. Does Epiq Discover support spinning up isolated, time-limited tenant workspaces (or sandbox projects) via API or admin tooling, or is every environment a manually provisioned instance that requires your operations team?

API surface for state validation — Find the **Senior Engineer or Tech Lead on the Discover API team**; they know the endpoint catalog. Does the Discover REST API expose read endpoints that can confirm discrete user actions inside a review workflow — e.g., verifying that a specific search was executed, a coding decision was applied, or a production set was created — so an external system could programmatically score learner progress?

NFR / training licensing model — Find the **VP of Alliances or Head of Sales Operations**; they control non-production licensing. Does Epiq offer NFR or training-tier licenses for Discover that allow a partner to run concurrent short-lived lab sessions (think 50-200 simultaneous users, each needing a pre-loaded document set), and if so, what is the per-seat or per-session cost structure?

account_intelligence.heading: Account Intelligence
bullets: Epiq serves Fortune 100 companies and AmLaw 100 law firms — likely requires compliance certifications (e.g., eDiscovery, legal tech) that could benefit from hands-on lab training, but current product (Discover™) is simulation-based with poor fit for Skillable's platform

VP of Customer Education role suggests mature training function, but organization's focus on legal services delivery means lab platform adoption would depend on whether they're building customer-facing certification programs rather than internal training

products.name: Epiq AI™
recommendation: Simulation is the only viable delivery path for Epiq AI™ because the product is a professional services practice group — not installable software — and Epiq Discover™ (the underlying platform) is cloud-hosted SaaS with no self-hosted option, no marketplace listing, and no documented per-learner provisioning API. Simulation labs can demonstrate AI-assisted eDiscovery workflows (Epiq Assist™ natural language queries, CAL configuration, production workflows) without requiring live platform access. The Epiq Discover™ certification exam (65 MCQ, 85% pass threshold) is the strongest anchor for a structured simulation-based practice experience.

MCQ scoring is confirmed viable and already in use for the Epiq Discover™ certification program — this is the natural starting point. AI Vision scoring can validate screen-state completion in guided simulation scenarios (e.g., confirming a learner reached the correct review workflow state). No programmatic API surface is available for automated outcome validation, so performance-based testing would require vendor engagement to expose a scoring API or webhook before PBT is viable.

The blocking question is: does Epiq offer a sandbox or training tenant for Epiq Discover™ that can be provisioned per learner without a full commercial contract? The team most likely to own this answer is the Epiq AI Labs group (VP: Bishan Yang) or the Epiq Discover product team. A verbatim question your champion can send: 'Do you have a sandbox environment or training tenant for Epiq Discover™ that supports per-learner provisioning for certification prep and hands-on training — and is there an API or automation path for standing up isolated learner environments at scale?'

The Epiq Discover™ End User Certification is the strongest program fit — upgrading the existing MCQ-only exam to include simulation-based practice labs would directly improve exam pass rates and learner confidence. A certification prep lab series (4-6 labs covering the exam domains) is the most defensible first investment. If Epiq opens a sandbox API, customer onboarding labs for new Epiq Discover™ clients are the natural second motion — reducing time-to-value for enterprise customers who pay significant fees for the platform.

Epiq AI™ as scored here is a professional services practice group with no standalone software to lab against — the instructional surface is advisory, not hands-on. The more labable product in Epiq's portfolio is Epiq Discover™ with Epiq Assist™ AI features, which has a real certification program, documented multi-phase workflows, and a defined user base. Any serious lab engagement with Epiq should be scoped to Epiq Discover™ rather than 'Epiq AI™' as a product name. Recommend re-scoring Epiq Discover™ as the primary product if vendor engagement proceeds.

dimensions.name: Provisioning
dimensions.summary: Epiq AI™ is a professional services practice group, not installable software. The underlying Epiq Discover™ platform is cloud-hosted SaaS with no self-hosted deployment option documented. No Azure/AWS marketplace listing found. Simulation is the only viable lab delivery path. No NFR or developer license tier identified.

Without a self-hosted deployment path, Hyper-V VM delivery is not viable.

key_technical_questions.heading: Key Technical Questions
bullets: API surface for Epiq AI workflows — Bishan Yang, VP of Epiq AI Labs, as the technical owner of the AI product. Does Epiq AI expose any REST or GraphQL API endpoints that allow external systems to programmatically submit documents, trigger AI-assisted review tasks, or retrieve classification/analysis results — or is all interaction confined to the browser UI with no programmatic access?

conversation_starters.heading: Conversation Starters
bullets: I'm curious how your team currently onboards users on Epiq AI — are they learning the decision logic and workflows through documentation, or do you have them working through real case scenarios where they can see how the AI recommendations actually perform?

A lot of legal tech teams tell us their biggest gap is helping users build confidence in *when* to trust the AI output versus when to override it — that kind of judgment call is hard to teach in a demo.

products.name: Epiq Assist™
recommendation: Simulation is the only viable delivery path for Epiq Assist™ without active vendor API cooperation — the product is SaaS-only with no self-hosted option, no marketplace deployment, and no documented per-learner provisioning API. A simulation built with AI Vision can demonstrate the natural language query workflow and document review interface, but cannot provide isolated per-learner data sets or real AI responses. This limits lab fidelity significantly compared to a real environment.

Scoring is limited to AI Vision screen-state evaluation and MCQ knowledge checks — no programmatic API surface exists for validating learner actions. This aligns with the existing Epiq Discover Certification model (MCQ-only, 65 questions), but limits the depth of performance-based assessment. If Epiq's product team can provide sandbox API access, script-based scoring of query results and document tagging actions would become viable.

The critical unresolved question is whether Epiq's product team can provide a sandbox API or isolated demo tenant with pre-loaded document sets for per-learner training use. The training or certification team is unlikely to own this answer — it requires the Epiq Discover product engineering team. The champion should ask: 'Can your product team provision isolated sandbox environments per learner via API, with pre-loaded document sets, for training and certification purposes? Is there a developer or partner sandbox program we can access?'

The existing Epiq Discover End User Certification is MCQ-only — adding a performance-based simulation component would strengthen credential value and differentiate the certification in the legal tech market. However, this requires either vendor API cooperation for real lab delivery or acceptance of simulation-only fidelity. The certification motion is the most natural Skillable entry point, but the technical blocker must be resolved first.

SaaS-only deployment with no documented per-learner provisioning API makes real lab delivery impossible without Epiq's active product team engagement. Do not invest significant solution engineering time until Epiq commits to providing sandbox API access or a per-learner isolation mechanism. Maintain the relationship at low cost — the certification program and AI adoption use case are genuine opportunities if the technical blocker is resolved.

dimensions.name: Provisioning
dimensions.summary: Epiq Assist™ is embedded in Epiq Discover™, a proprietary SaaS platform with no self-hosted deployment option, no Azure/AWS marketplace listing, and no documented per-learner provisioning API. Simulation is the only viable delivery path. GUI-only interaction and absence of an NFR license tier further constrain the provisioning score.

evidence.claim: **Delivery Method | Context:** Epiq Assist™ is embedded in Epiq Discover™, a proprietary SaaS platform with no self-hosted deployment, no Azure/AWS marketplace listing, and no documented per-learner provisioning API — simulation is the only viable delivery path without active vendor API cooperation. — Epiq Technology page (epiqglobal.com). This means no real isolated lab environment can be provisioned per learner without Epiq's product team engagement.

**SaaS Lock-In | Blocker:** No self-hosted, on-premises, or cloud-marketplace deployment option is documented for Epiq Discover™ or Epiq Assist™ — the platform runs exclusively on Epiq's proprietary infrastructure, making per-learner environment provisioning impossible without vendor API access. — Epiq Discover product page (epiqglobal.com). This is the primary technical blocker for real lab delivery.

**No VM Path | Blocker:** Epiq Assist™ has no installable component — it is a cloud-hosted AI feature within a managed SaaS platform. There is no Windows or Linux installer, no OVA, and no container image available, ruling out Hyper-V, ESX, and container delivery entirely. — Epiq Discover product page (epiqglobal.com). Simulation is the only path.

dimensions.name: Lab Access
dimensions.summary: No per-learner isolation mechanism is documented. Access requires an Epiq-provisioned account on their hosted platform. No credential pool, no Entra ID SSO, no NFR accounts confirmed. Shared demo tenant access is the best realistic scenario, with no programmatic account creation path.

evidence.claim: **No NFR License | Risk:** No non-production license tier, developer sandbox, or free trial is documented for Epiq Discover™ or Epiq Assist™ — access requires a commercial engagement with Epiq, making it impossible to provision lab accounts without vendor cooperation. — Epiq Discover product page (epiqglobal.com). Vendor engagement is required before any lab authoring can begin.

**No Self-Service Trial | Risk:** No self-service trial or demo account is publicly available for Epiq Discover™ — the product page directs prospects to 'Talk to an Expert,' indicating all access is gated behind a sales conversation. — Epiq Discover product page (epiqglobal.com). This eliminates the trial account path for lab access.

dimensions.name: Scoring
dimensions.summary: No programmatic API surface for validating learner actions. Simulation scoring via guided interaction checkpoints and AI Vision screen-state evaluation are the only viable methods. The existing Epiq Discover Certification uses MCQ-only (65 questions), confirming the absence of a performance-based scoring surface.

evidence.claim: **Existing Cert Model | Context:** The Epiq Discover End User Certification uses 65 multiple-choice and true/false questions with a 70-minute time limit and 85% passing score — confirming that MCQ is the current and only validated scoring method for this platform. — Epiq Discover Certification page (epiqglobal.com). Any Skillable lab would align with this MCQ-only model unless vendor API access is obtained.

**Simulation Scoring | Opportunity:** Epiq Assist™'s natural language query interface is entirely GUI-driven — AI Vision can evaluate screen state (query entered, results surfaced, documents tagged) to provide richer scoring than MCQ alone in a simulation environment. — Epiq Discover product page (epiqglobal.com). AI Vision is the best available scoring upgrade over the existing MCQ-only certification.

dimensions.name: Teardown
dimensions.summary: Simulation environments have no persistent state to clean up. No orphan risk, no API teardown required. Teardown is effectively automatic — simulation sessions end cleanly with no residual resources.

evidence.claim: **Simulation Teardown | Strength:** Simulation environments have no persistent state — sessions end cleanly with no residual resources, orphaned accounts, or cleanup required. Teardown is effectively automatic and never a build concern for simulation-based labs. — Skillable platform architecture (inferred from simulation delivery model). No teardown engineering effort required.

dimensions.name: Product Complexity
dimensions.summary: Epiq Assist™ is a natural language AI query interface — the interaction model is conversational and designed to reduce complexity, not expose it. The underlying Epiq Discover™ platform has more depth (review workflows, tagging, production, CAL), but Assist™ itself is an AI abstraction layer. AI-embedded feature learning adds some hands-on value, but the interface is intentionally simple. Limited configuration depth constrains the complexity score.

evidence.claim: **AI Query Interface | Opportunity:** Epiq Assist™ enables natural language queries across entire eDiscovery datasets — including deposition prep (5x faster), early case assessment, and internal investigations — requiring iterative hands-on practice to use effectively in real legal matters. — Epiq Discover product page (epiqglobal.com). AI-embedded feature learning is the primary instructional value driver for this product.

**Workflow Breadth | Context:** Epiq Discover™ covers multiple eDiscovery phases — collection, processing, early case assessment, review, and production — but Epiq Assist™ is an AI overlay on these phases, not a deep configuration tool. The underlying platform has workflow depth; the AI assistant simplifies rather than exposes it. — Epiq Discover product page (epiqglobal.com). Lab scenarios are limited to the AI query interaction layer, not deep platform configuration.

**Intentionally Simple Interface | Risk:** Epiq Assist™ is designed to reduce complexity — users type natural language questions and receive AI-surfaced answers. The interaction model is conversational, not configurable, limiting the depth of hands-on lab scenarios that can be built around the product. — Epiq Discover product page (epiqglobal.com). Simple UX reduces the range of meaningful lab tasks.

dimensions.name: Mastery Stakes
dimensions.summary: Legal professionals face genuine high stakes: missing critical documents in litigation carries malpractice and sanctions risk. However, Epiq Assist™ is designed to reduce the skill burden through AI — the stakes are real but the tool is designed to be intuitive. AI adoption risk in legal is a documented challenge, and hands-on practice directly addresses adoption hesitancy.

evidence.claim: **Litigation Risk | Strength:** Missing critical documents in eDiscovery due to poor query formulation or misuse of AI tools can result in sanctions, adverse inference rulings, or malpractice exposure — making competent use of Epiq Assist™ genuinely high-stakes for legal professionals. — Epiq Discover product page (epiqglobal.com). High stakes justify training investment even for a relatively simple interface.

**AI Adoption in Legal | Opportunity:** AI adoption in legal services is a documented industry challenge — attorneys and legal ops professionals are cautious about trusting AI-surfaced results without understanding how to validate them. Hands-on simulation labs directly address adoption hesitancy by building confidence through practice. — Epiq AI Practice Group page (epiqglobal.com). Adoption risk is the strongest training demand signal for this product.

dimensions.name: Lab Versatility
dimensions.summary: One lab type applies: Compliance Audit — validating document review configurations against regulatory requirements. The natural language query interface does not lend itself to Break/Fix or adversarial scenarios. Lab versatility is narrow given the conversational AI interaction model.

evidence.claim: **Regulatory Review Scenario | Opportunity:** Epiq Discover™ supports customizable document review tagging for state and local regulatory compliance — a Compliance Audit lab scenario where learners validate document categorization against regulatory requirements is a natural fit for legal and compliance professionals. — Epiq Discover product page (epiqglobal.com). This is the strongest lab type for this product given the regulatory context.

evidence.claim: **Legal Tech Category | Context:** Legal Tech is a moderate-demand training category with growing AI adoption pressure — law firms and corporate legal departments are actively investing in AI tools to reduce outside counsel spend and accelerate case resolution. — Epiq Technology page (epiqglobal.com). Category demand is moderate, not high — legal tech does not have the same training ecosystem depth as cybersecurity or cloud infrastructure.

The existing certification is a natural entry point for adding performance-based lab components if vendor API access is obtained.

key_technical_questions.heading: Key Technical Questions
bullets: API surface for Epiq Assist — Find the **Principal Engineer or Tech Lead on the Epiq Assist product team** (Engineering/Product). "Does Epiq Assist expose any REST or GraphQL APIs that allow external systems to programmatically create a workspace, upload a document set, trigger an AI-assisted review workflow, and retrieve review results or status — or is all interaction confined to the browser UI with no external automation hooks?"

Sandbox or NFR tenant provisioning — Find the **Director of Partnerships or Head of Sales Engineering** (Go-to-Market/Alliances). "Can Epiq provision short-lived, isolated Epiq Assist tenants (or sandbox environments) on demand for training or certification purposes — and if so, is there a programmatic way to spin up and tear down those environments, or does each instance require manual provisioning by your team?"

Scoring and state validation — Find the **Senior Product Manager or Solutions Architect for Epiq Assist** (Product/Engineering). "Within an Epiq Assist review workflow, is there any mechanism — API endpoint, webhook, audit log export, or event stream — that an external system could query to confirm a learner completed specific actions (e.g., applied a coding tag, ran a search, accepted or rejected AI suggestions), or are user actions only visible within the platform's own reporting UI?"
